tracking.controllers: Valve Index Controllers / Vive Wands / Vive Tracker

knownIssues.title: Requires custom-fitted facial interface — no adjustable IPD ring
knownIssues.description: The Beyond 2, like its predecessor, uses a custom 3D-printed facial interface made from a face scan. Incorrect fit degrades optical clarity significantly. IPD is fixed per-order.
knownIssues.workaround: Complete the Bigscreen face scan process accurately. If your IPD changes or the fit is poor, contact Bigscreen for a replacement interface. Do not share the headset without a properly fitted interface for each user.

knownIssues.title: Micro-OLED panels can exhibit burn-in with static content
knownIssues.description: Like all OLED displays, the Beyond 2's Micro-OLED panels are susceptible to image retention if static UI elements are displayed for extended periods.
knownIssues.workaround: Avoid pausing in VR with static HUDs visible for more than 15-20 minutes. Enable SteamVR's display timeout. Do not use the headset as a VR desktop monitor for hours at a time.

knownIssues.title: SteamVR Lighthouse base stations required — sold separately
knownIssues.description: The Beyond 2 has no built-in inside-out tracking and requires SteamVR Lighthouse base stations (version 1.0 or 2.0) and compatible controllers, none of which are included.
knownIssues.workaround: Purchase SteamVR 2.0 base stations (2 required for full-room tracking) and compatible controllers (Valve Index, Vive Wand, or VIVE Ultimate). Factor this into total cost.

optimizationTips.tip: Micro-OLED delivers exceptional contrast — embrace dark game environments
optimizationTips.simpleExplanation: The Beyond 2's screen has true black pixels and stunning color — dark VR games look dramatically better than on any LCD headset.
optimizationTips.advancedExplanation: Micro-OLED provides per-pixel light emission with infinite contrast ratio. In dark scenes, black levels are orders of magnitude deeper than LCD. HDR-aware games benefit most. Use in-game HDR settings where available. Avoid heavy vignette post-processing which masks the true blacks.

optimizationTips.tip: 90Hz with SteamVR motion smoothing is the target operating point
optimizationTips.simpleExplanation: Set your refresh rate to 90Hz and let SteamVR handle smoothing when needed. This is the sweet spot for the Beyond 2.
optimizationTips.advancedExplanation: The Beyond 2 at 90Hz with 2560x2560 per eye is demanding but manageable on RTX 4080+ hardware. SteamVR motion smoothing at 45fps→90Hz is highly effective on this headset due to the predictable Lighthouse tracking. Use per-game resolution scaling to maintain 45fps floor.

optimizationTips.tip: The Beyond 2 is ultra-lightweight — use a halo strap for long sessions
optimizationTips.simpleExplanation: The headset is very light but the minimal design means you'll want a halo-style strap for comfort in sessions over 30 minutes.
optimizationTips.advancedExplanation: The Beyond 2 weighs under 150g making it the lightest full-featured SteamVR headset. However, the minimal design puts pressure on the nose bridge without a halo strap. Third-party halo straps distribute weight to the forehead, enabling multi-hour sessions without facial pressure.
